items.title: Protect .htaccess and configuration files
items.description: Prevent direct access to sensitive files containing configuration data such as database credentials.
items.threat: Information Disclosure - Attackers can read configuration files to obtain database credentials.
items.impact: Complete site compromise, database breach, data theft.

implementation.apache:
<FilesMatch "^\.(ht|ini|phps|fla|psd|log|sh)">
  Require all denied
</FilesMatch>

<Files "configuration.php">
  Require all denied
</Files>
implementation.nginx:
location ~* /\.(ht|ini|phps|fla|psd|log|sh)$ { deny all; return 403; }

location ~ configuration.php { deny all; return 403; }
implementation.litespeed:
<FilesMatch "^\.(ht|ini|phps|fla|psd|log|sh)">
  Require all denied
</FilesMatch>

<Files "configuration.php">
  Require all denied
</Files>

mitigationSteps: 2. Add FilesMatch directive to block sensitive extensions
4. Test by attempting to access configuration.php directly
5. Monitor server error logs for blocked attempts
items.rollback: Remove the FilesMatch and Files blocks in .htaccess or nginx config.

items.title: Change default administrator username
items.description: Never use 'admin' as super user. Create new super user with unique name and delete the default.
items.threat: Brute Force Attacks - Default usernames are the first target for automated login attacks.
items.impact: Unauthorized admin access, site defacement, malware injection.

implementation.apache:
# Users > Manage > Add New User
# Set Super Users group
# Logout and login with new account
# Delete old 'admin' user

mitigationSteps: 1. Login to Joomla admin with existing admin account
4. Fill: Name, Username (unique, 12+ chars), Email, Password
6. Logout and login with new account
testing: Verify new super user can access all admin functions
items.rollback: Create another super user via database if needed.

items.title: Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
items.description: Require MFA/TOTP for all administrator accounts using built-in Joomla MFA.
items.threat: Credential Theft - Stolen passwords can be used if no second factor required.
items.impact: Full admin compromise even with strong password.
items.verification: Login prompts for MFA code after password

implementation.apache:
# System > Manage > Users > Multi-factor Authentication
# Enable for backend users

mitigationSteps: 4. Each user sets up MFA in their profile
5. Scan QR code with authenticator app
testing: Login with password - should request MFA
Try wrong MFA code - should fail
items.rollback: Disable MFA from user profile or use backup codes.

items.title: Keep Joomla Core updated
items.description: Keep Joomla core updated to patch security vulnerabilities using Stable Update Channel.
items.threat: Exploit of Known Vulnerabilities - Old versions have public exploits.
items.impact: Remote code execution, data breach, complete site compromise.
items.verification: No available updates in System > Update > Joomla

implementation.apache:
# System > Update > Joomla
# Check for Updates
# Install latest stable version

mitigationSteps: 3. Read release notes for breaking changes
testing: Check System > System Information shows latest version
items.rollback: Restore from backup if site breaks after update.

items.title: Use strong database credentials
items.description: Database user should have complex password (20+ chars) with minimal privileges.
items.threat: Database Compromise - Weak passwords can be brute forced.
items.impact: Data theft, data corruption, site compromise.
items.verification: Password length >20 chars with random characters

implementation.apache:
# Generate: openssl rand -base64 24
# Update in configuration.php

items.title: Enable HTTPS for entire site
items.description: Force SSL for all frontend and backend pages.
items.threat: Man-in-the-Middle Attacks - HTTP traffic can be intercepted.
items.impact: Session hijacking, credential theft, data theft.
items.verification: All URLs redirect to https:// and show secure padlock

implementation.apache:
# System > Global Configuration > Server
# Force HTTPS: Administrator & Frontend
implementation.litespeed:
# System > Global Configuration > Server
# Force HTTPS: Administrator & Frontend

mitigationSteps: 6. Update all internal links to https://
testing: SSL Labs rating A+ or A
items.rollback: Remove SSL configuration and disable Force HTTPS.

items.title: Remove /installation directory
items.description: Delete the /installation directory immediately after Joomla installation is complete.
items.threat: Re-installation Attack - Attackers can re-run installer and compromise site.
items.impact: Complete site compromise, database overwrite, unauthorized access.
items.verification: Directory does not exist and returns 404
testing: ls -la installation/ should return 'No such file'
items.rollback: Cannot restore - reinstall Joomla if needed.

items.title: Disable Debug Mode and Error Reporting
items.description: Disable Debug Mode, Debug Language, and set Error Reporting to 'None' in production.
items.threat: Information Disclosure - Debug messages reveal sensitive paths and data.
items.impact: Attackers gain knowledge of system internals, file paths, database queries.
items.verification: No debug messages or errors displayed on frontend

implementation.apache:
# System > Global Configuration > System
# Debug Settings: No
# Error Reporting: None

testing: Visit frontend with invalid URL - no stack traces shown
items.rollback: Re-enable debug mode in Global Configuration.

items.title: Remove unused extensions
items.description: Uninstall extensions (components, modules, plugins) that are not in use.
items.threat: Vulnerable Extensions - Unused extensions may have unpatched vulnerabilities.
items.impact: Remote code execution, site compromise through extension vulnerabilities.

implementation.apache:
# System > Manage > Extensions
# Filter by type and uninstall

mitigationSteps: 2. Filter by Components, Modules, Plugins, Libraries
testing: Extensions list shows only needed items
items.rollback: Reinstall extension from backup or Joomla Extensions.

items.title: Secure configuration.php Permissions
items.description: Set ownership and permissions for configuration.php to prevent unauthorized access.
items.threat: Unauthorized Configuration Access - Wrong permissions allow reading configuration.
items.verification: File permissions are 644 or 640 and owned by web server user
